pending.approvalReason: EROS-internal SEO tactic (schema / content / on-page). No budget, no external access, no legal disclosure. EROS activates without waiting for explicit YES.

pending.source: data/research/2026-05-18/findings.md (Matt Diamante (HeyTony) — 2026 Local SEO Best Practices)
pending.sectionTitle: Matt Diamante (HeyTony) — 2026 Local SEO Best Practices
pending.title: Systematic GBP review-request workflow
pending.detail: Systematic GBP review-request workflow. Sean's network has 30+ GBPs with low review counts. A monthly automated "review request" email/SMS to served customers (released defendants + their indemnitors, with permission) would compound review volume across the network. Matt Diamante's data point: reviews are the strongest local-rank correlation factor for service businesses. Currently we have zero EROS automation for this.

suggestedTestDesign.controlScope: 2 sister B-tier sites (matched on traffic volume)

pending.approvalReason: Legal/consent risk requires explicit Angelo approval.

pending.source: data/research/2026-05-18/findings.md (Hyperlocal SEO for Bail Bonds — Post-2018 Google Ads Ban)
pending.sectionTitle: Hyperlocal SEO for Bail Bonds — Post-2018 Google Ads Ban
pending.title: Bing Ads testing for bail bonds
pending.detail: Bing Ads testing for bail bonds. Bing's policies allow bail-bond advertising (Google bans, Bing doesn't). Even at 8-10% search market share, a $200-500/mo Bing Ads test against the 2 A-tier sites could produce measurable lift since competition is artificially low. Worth a strategy test under the existing Strategy Timeline framework — 28-day window, treatment vs control.

suggestedTestDesign.controlScope: 2 sister B-tier sites (matched on traffic volume)

pending.approvalReason: Requires cash budget ($200-500/mo per site). Angelo approval mandatory.

pending.source: data/research/2026-05-18/findings.md (Hyperlocal SEO for Bail Bonds — Post-2018 Google Ads Ban)
pending.sectionTitle: Hyperlocal SEO for Bail Bonds — Post-2018 Google Ads Ban
pending.title: Directory submission sprint
pending.detail: Directory submission sprint. The 25% "secondary channel" allocation that GetMoreBonds recommends is something we have not systematically done. Lawyers.com, Avvo.com, Justia, BBB, FindLaw, Yelp, Yellow Pages — each requires unique content per listing. Auto-deferral risk because it's tedious work, but the citation signal is documented to feed local map pack rankings.

suggestedTestDesign.controlScope: 2 sister B-tier sites (matched on traffic volume)

pending.approvalReason: Needs Sean/Angelo-controlled input (bio, license, account, time). Angelo approval mandatory.
